text: See ya' round.
Vald? Why would you want to help that idiot?
He owes me a steep debt. In fact, he's lucky all I did was force him to work for Mercer.
Really? I wrote that off a long time ago.
Well, I suppose I need to fulfill my end of the bargain.
Give this document to Vald. It frees him from the debt, but I never want to see him in Riften again.
I had Vald meet the caravan from Winterhold on the western end of the lake so he could avoid the roads.
The idiot got lost in the mist and ran his boat right over some rocks, sending it straight to the bottom.
He's too stupid to remember where he was, and any attempt I've made to find it in the past has been unsuccessful.
Wow, sure is nice of ya'. Here, hang on to the key and don't let anyone in!
Vald said his rowboat struck some rocks... I'd check under the water near one of the small islands in the lake.
Perhaps you should start at the docks and work your way out towards Goldenglow Estate.
Just find a way in, get the information and leave. And you have permission to kill anyone that stands in your way.
But I can't leave here! I'm supposed to watch his house. What do I do?
Oh come on, I ain't that dumb.
Be careful, lass. This is the last place in Skyrim I'd ever want to send you.
Fortunately, the quill required an ink with a unique formula known only to myself.
So if you're getting any ideas about selling it to someone else or keeping it, you'll find it would hold little value.
Do what he says, Vex. This ain't helpin' right now.
The quill was supposed to allow me to duplicate any writing I'm viewing in exacting detail.
I'm sure you can understand how useful that would prove in a business situation, especially where signatures were involved.
Fine. We do it your way. For now.
I don't know how ya' talked Maven into letting me go, and I don't care.
You did something for me, I'm gonna do something for you. Here, take the key to Mercer's house. Won't need it any more.
Let me see.
Look for a way out! We are not going to burn to death in our own damned Sanctuary!
Come on! We've got to find a way out of here!
Oh dear. That wasn't supposed to happen.
Well, I... I simply thought you might wish to help out one of your fellow mages.
If that's not enough, I suppose... I have this amulet you could take.
This really isn't turning out the way I'd hoped.
There! Now, I...
Let me try again...
Please hold still, and don't move.
We're all trying to become better mages, right?
Well, I need someone I can practice a few spells on. Nothing dangerous, really. Would you be willing to assist me?
I still need someone to practice a few spells on. Interested?
Oh no, that's not it at all.
Oh. Well, if you change your mind, let me know.
There! All better.
I'm not really sure. I think it's just a minor miscalculation on my part.
I'm sure it will wear off soon. When it does, you'll be perfectly fine, and then we can try again.
In the meantime, I'll see about figuring out what went wrong.
Just wait. Just... I can fix this.
It's not!
It shouldn't be. You'll be back to normal in no time.
And when you are, we can try this again and I'll be sure to get it right.
I still need some help, if you can spare the time.
If you have a moment, could you help me with something?
This will only take me a moment...
Oh my. That isn't right!
I am so very sorry. I went over this again and again, and I was sure it would have better results.
Oh. J'zargo takes this to mean it did not go... well.
This was not the intention, to be sure. It is true that J'zargo was slightly concerned there may have been... problems.
But you were not placed in harm's way on purpose.
Still, you are a good friend to J'zargo now, for taking this risk. J'zargo thanks you.
Okay, now don't move at all.
Oh, good. I was afraid I'd have to go ask J'zargo.
Now, you just stand there...
Oh, good. I was afraid I'd have to rely on Onmund.
Now, you just stand there...
Thank you. I was afraid I'd have to figure out some way to do this on my own.
Now, you just stand there...
No?
It is true, J'zargo suspected there might be problems. But this is most disappointing.
It is good that you were willing to help J'zargo. This will not be forgotten, friend.
And? Were they magnificent?
You have to hold still or this isn't going to work.
No, not that kind of spell. Believe me when I say I wouldn't be asking if it weren't necessary.
Ah. No, that was not supposed to happen.
But J'zargo is pleased to see that you are here, and not in pieces!
You are a good friend to have done this. J'zargo will not forget it.
Do you... do you feel all right? You look very, umm, green.
I'll get it right this time, I swear.
This is gettin' dangerous. Don't let anythin' get the jump on you.
While you're in there, help yourself to anything in Frey's manor. I would.
Careful at Mercer's place, I don't want to lose anyone else to that madman.
I'll watch for Mercer, just get the information we need.
Come on, we need to show the journal to the Guild.
Can't talk long, got to keep my eyes open for Mercer.
Hold your thoughts until after we untangle this mess.
Need to stay focused in case Frey comes back. Make it fast.
How can I help?
I can't believe this is happening, lass.
Just take a step back and let me figure this out. Then we'll talk.
You had your chance to talk!
Please be careful here. The site isn't entirely secured.
Do you think there's treasure here? Something to make J'zargo a powerful wizard?
Who knows what we might find in here.
I'm not seeing signs of any warding magics at all. Either they're not here, or they were removed long ago.
This is better than sitting around talking about magic, don't you think?
Can you imagine what it was like, living here so long ago? The first men in Tamriel, here...
I'm not so sure we should be here.
Svari, yes. It's been hard on her. The girl misses her uncle.
I've tried to explain that Roggvir did something bad and that we all have to be accountable for our actions but... a child feels what she will.
Because the Dominion is a sleeping beast that Skyrim cannot slay alone. Because many Nords are part of the Imperial army even now.
Because the food and resources we get from the Empire are important to our people.
Because even if we can't openly worship him, Talos the god was once Tiber Septim the man, and this is his Empire.
And Torygg wasn't ready to let it fall apart.
Not much. But as a bard, I find the whole affair depressing.
There are no heroes in this war. No winners to be had and no real conclusion.
Well, it's a great big festival put on by the bards every year. They burn an effigy of old King Olaf. It's quite a bit of fun.
The bards give out Spiced Wine during the festival, so they put in a big order. And then, for weeks after, demand is so high, I can barely keep up.
But with the death of High King Torygg, the festival has been declared "in poor taste." So it looks like it won't happen this year.
They're... adequate. Honestly, I rely on the business brought in by the Burning of King Olaf, and that's not likely to happen this year.
Oh, yes. A secret family recipe. The only good thing my father Octieve ever did. In fact, it's so good, no one else in Skyrim even tries to make any.
No. Even after Istlod died, the Moot voted to make Torygg High King of Skyrim.
But Ulfric was at that moot, continually talking about Skyrim's independence in terms just shy of treason.
I don't think Ulfric knew how much Torygg respected him for that.
If Ulfric had asked Torygg directly to stand up, to declare independence, Torygg might have done it.
Ulfric showed up at the gates of Solitude requesting an audience.
We thought he was here to ask Torygg to declare independence. By the time we realized Ulfric was here to challenge Torygg... it was already too late.
Because Ulfric needed a symbol.
Someone he could defeat that represented the Empire, the White-Gold Concordat, the banning of Talos worship.
Torygg's father Istlod had held Skyrim together for nearly twenty-five years. When he died, Torygg became that symbol.
I was, to my shame. The whole court was in attendance. I've seen much in my time, but that was a gruesome day.
That I do. I had a messenger from Dragon Bridge stop here on his way to the Blue Palace, not too long ago.
He was very nervous, mentioned something about needing help.
At times. Jaree-Ra stopped by the other day. Told me to keep an eye out for him, see if any new people arrived in the city.
If we send half our guards to aid the legions, just for a few days, it could tip the scales in the Empire's favor.
The Companions can help protect the city, and the Emperor will be in our debt.
I haven't changed my mind, if that's what you're asking. I won't leave Whiterun defenseless, even for a day.
Have you reconsidered my idea?
Have you lost all sense? We are short on guards as it is!
If we lose half our men, the Dark Brotherhood and Thieves Guild will have the run of the city. The Stormcloaks will be the least of our worries!
The answer is no, I tell you! Now stop pestering me with your warmongering!
Everyone who's anyone. Viarmo bought an apple the other day, said he was looking for a new student.
Not lately. Business has been slow.
Halt!
You have no business here, so keep walking.
Not much, to be honest. Their return was a shock to us all. Giraud Gemane has some tomes about them in the library, if you're interested.
I like to think I've aged well. I'm certainly wiser than I once was. And I know when one should speak... and when one should hold their tongue.
Watch that tongue, new blood.
Come, follow me.
By Nord custom, once the challenge was issued in court, Torygg had no choice but to accept.
Had he not, Ulfric would have had cause to call a new moot and a new vote for high king.
Torygg had some martial training, of course, but it mattered little that day.
When Ulfric's lips parted, when he unleashed the power of the Thu'um... That Shout, that ancient and terrible tongue... ripped Torygg asunder.
Very. I helped raise him. Oh, I could not have been more proud to see Torygg on the throne. He made a fine king. A fine king...
You have asked for my help. I am providing it.
Mmm, sorry. I really can't risk it. I'm sure you can find someone else with a little less dignity.
Watch this.
Go ahead and play your little joke.
Sorry, but you'll have to find someone else to help you with your prank.
Don't worry. I'll play my part.
Yes, this probably isn't really the place for your little jokes.
Thank you. I knew you'd understand.
I'll try to forget you ever asked.
I'm sorry, but I really can't.
I'm doing as you asked. This is for some good purpose, yes?
Tell your men to stand down. I don't need any help dealing with this kind of fool. He isn't worth the trouble.
I apologize for the disruption to such a festive occasion.
Dear me. All this trouble over me? I think I was confused. There's no harm in him. Please, let him go.
Oh... I'm afraid I may have overreacted. Please, I've already forgotten whatever it was he said to me.
I'm sure he's learned his lesson. Let's try to enjoy ourselves.
Be about your business, while I'm about mine.
Begone, serpent! Begone from this house and trouble it no more!
Beware of the serpent in your midst! Beware, oh people of Skyrim!
There's no excuse for your behavior!
You behave like you're carousing in a house of ill repute, not the residence of the Thalmor ambassador!
You're a disgrace to the Empire. To think that you're actually the representative of the East Empire Company.
When people talk about the decadent, tottering Empire, it's people like you they have in mind!
Go ahead and play your joke.
Say no more. I was wondering what you were doing here... I'm glad to play my part.
I trust you don't presume on our friendship. I'm a jarl, after all. I wouldn't want to appear foolish in front of these people.
Hmm. I don't want to get involved in anything beneath my dignity as jarl.
Oh... perhaps not, then. I don't think the Jarl of Solitude should be participating in a childish prank.
I don't know... I don't want to do anything that might offend Elenwen. There is a lot of money at stake, after all.
It's all for a good cause, old chap.
Ulfric Stormcloak? The rightful high king? That's bordering on treason, man! Stand up. Stand up, I say!
What...! Do you know who I am? I should have you taken up and flogged!
You've got a lot of nerve, suggesting something like that, to me of all people! If this was Riften, I'd have you thrown in the dungeon!
You asked for my help. Not for nothing, I hope?
I'm doing as you asked. Not for nothing, I hope?
Razelan. What is it this time?
I don't understand... did you say Ulfric Stormcloak? Fine fellow, although a bit too fond of... I mean, no, of course, I condemn him in the most...
Hmm? Did you say snakes? Where? I hate them, always slithering the way they do... What, are they on me?
What? Of course I know who you are... I didn't mean to suggest, that is, I don't remember even saying that...
Let's hear it.
Well... let's hear it.
My ears are always open to a friend's request.
Say on, friend. What do you need?
I'm always glad to help a friend if I can. What do you need?
This is preposterous. Disrespect... insulting! I am tired of being hounded from all sides!
Madame Ambassador, you know that I would never have behaved... that is to say... well, I swear I'm innocent this time!
I've got this. Do what you need to do.
I hope you're enjoying the music, sir.
I hope you're enjoying the music, ma'am.
These parties are a great way to earn a little extra money.
It's good practice. Most people barely notice what I'm playing.
I hope I get into the Bards College one day.
Well, I really would like to make sure I've corrected what happened last time.
Please give me a chance to try again.
Well, it all worked out in the end, didn't it?
Good, good. It'll be worth it, I assure you.
This will be worth it.
Yes, but it needs to be kept quiet.
I may have entered into an... agreement with Enthir. He had something I needed, so I traded him something of mine.
It was a mistake, and now I want back what I gave him. It's an amulet that belonged to my family, and I never should've given it to Enthir.
But he won't deal with me. Talk to him; see what it'll take to get my amulet back.
Exactly as I said it would.
Now, are you ready to try again? I am confident it will work this time.
I need your help with something. Something private.
Oh, no it hasn't. Let's be honest.
I have a long way to go before I'm ready for the kind of advanced magic I've been attempting.
But at least I know where to start now. Thank you, you've been a great help to me.
Agreed.
And thank you for your help. You're a good friend.
Damn outsider. Why couldn't you just mind your own business?
You're no liar. Best fight I've had in years. There you are.
If you ever need another blade at your side, just ask. Wouldn't mind seeing how you handle a few trolls.
Every day the threat of exile from Riften draws closer for myself and my family.
Who knows how many spies the Empire has sent into our midst already. We're at war. This isn't the time for hospitality.
Thank goodness Mother is keeping a level head about everything. If my brother Saerlund had his way, we'd be flying Imperial colors by now.
Ulfric only cares about one thing... Ulfric. He's ordained himself the future High King of Skyrim and steps on anyone that gets in his way.
He's begun a rebellion against those that wish to eradicate the worship of Talos and uses it as his rallying cry.
His cause may be true, but the man is a lie... all he holds in his heart is lust for the throne.
Because I did something not a soul in this bloodstained house of war has the backbone to do... dared to speak my mind.
I dared speak of the Empire and the lies that have been spread by Ulfric, the leader of the Stormcloaks.
Now my mother's stripped me of my heritage and incarcerated me here like a common criminal and my brother has all but disowned me.
Be wary what you say around here, friend. You'll find not all take kindly to "insurrection."
Good. Wait until I start the distraction, then show me what you're made of.
Can you believe that fool had the audacity to speak of his love for the Empire in the plaza?
He expects us to drop our defenses and greet them with open arms... to dialogue with them rather than defend our homeland by spilling their blood.
He's a traitor, plain and simple. Had I been sitting on the throne, he'd be hanging from the gallows for his sympathies towards the Empire.
I know it looks bad, but we've got to figure out what happened. And if that dragon is still skulking around somewhere.
It's not an insult, just an observation.
If I were walking around with a wound, I would want you to tell me to see a doctor.
Your outfit is something of an open wound... where attire is concerned.
Not many people care, these days. The world is the worse for it.
We mostly carry herbs and alchemical ingredients, that sort of thing. Before the war, our stock was mostly used for aromatic use in homes, yes.
These days, we have more people like you coming in, looking for healing and the like.
I suppose I would, but you're the only one who gets that greeting.
It's never been an issue. People say you helped with the problems at Wolfskull Cave.
I applaud anyone who assists the court.
Oh, we do all right. Rumor is, you had a hand in the Blackblood Marauders' demise...
I'm impressed.
Radiant Raiment has the finest attire in all of Skyrim. That is how we...
Wait. You're that new bard. The one who saved the festival, aren't you?
That was a good bit of work.
You should look into joining. They could use every hand they can get.
But I'm not telling you anything you don't already know.
No, no. Mostly, I'm just a simple herbalist. Lami over in Morthal should be able to help you.
No, no. I'm just a simple herbalist. You'll want to see Arcadia, over in Whiterun.
It's almost all I have time for these days.
The men training here in Solitude and the troops out in the field, they all need weapons and armor.
Only when you're around. The rest of these fools I can do without.
You'd think so, wouldn't you? I'm fine. Thank you for asking.
It is when my customers waste time chatting, and not buying.
I think I would, if it weren't for my son. And my wife.
Oh, Sayma would have my hide if I even suggested I might step foot on a battlefield.
You think Stormcloaks and Legionnaires are dangerous? Ha!
Nothing at all. It's very... nice. If you want something with a little more style, however, you should come by our shop. Radiant Raiment.
We could fix you right up.
I do. So few people possess the taste and sophistication of a proper wardrobe these days.
Be sure and investigate Radiant Raiment's fine selection of clothing. We have many items you'll fancy, I'm sure.
As high priest of the Nine Divines, I bid you welcome.
We welcome you to worship the Nine Divines, freely and openly.
Blessings be with you.
Blessings of the Nine Divines upon you. How may I help?
May the Divines guide and keep you.
May you be saved by the Divines, as I once was.
I hid here in Solitude for a long time, but war... war follows a man until it's done with him.
You shouldn't leave Solitude without trying our fish. Best in Skyrim.
Mother says I shouldn't talk to people...
If you want to talk about my brother, I'm not interested.
So good to see you again. How can I be of service?
No. That was an ugly affair. High King Torygg's death has turned the whole town on its head.
Between you and me? He wasn't even that good a king. All of those rambling speeches about the Empire this, and the Empire that...
We sure showed them, eh? Thanks for your part in all that.
Good job out there. Glad you were with us.
Thanks. Couldn't have done that without you.
Kind of fun to push people around sometimes, isn't it? Good work, sister.
You seem to have a knack for this kind of thing. I'll make sure people in charge know.
I figured you had it in you. Nicely done.
Try not to get carried away.
Get to it, then.
Not bad, not bad. The intensity of the hunt is special, isn't it?
Good to hear. Have your share of the pay.
But I don't want to be patient! I want my dress right now! Father promised!
It takes a while to make a dress, Dagny. You have to be patient.
I'm so angry, I can hardly stand it!
Father promised me a new dress days ago. Now where is it?
Damn right it has. Demand's never been so high, at least not since I built the new mill.
Leave me out of your intrigues, Hrongar. If you have something to say to the jarl, say it with your own tongue.
My husband owns it, barely. He'll lose it soon enough. He's behind on his loan payments, but he drinks all his money away just the same.
Now we're deep in debt, penniless and about to lose our sole source of income.
I'm not asking you to defy the jarl, just to open his eyes.
Use subtlety and suggestion to turn his thoughts to the war. You're his steward, he'll listen to you.
No, I openly welcome the presence of armed strangers who have no reason to care about any of us.
Of course it bothers me! I'm sure it's only a matter of time until they seize our mill in the name of their war effort.
Next thing you know, there'll be more guards, and then we'll lose our homes as well. This is just the start of it, mark my words.
What would you have me do, Hrongar? Your brother is the jarl. You know I can't question his judgement.
Proventus, you must help me. We need to convince my brother to do more for the war effort.
I'm going to tell Father you said that. He'll tan your hide for sure!
All you ever do is complain about what you want. You're a spoiled baby.
This isn't an order of cowards. I'll find someone more agreeable, I'm sure.
Be cautious. Beasts are not nearly as predictable as men. Give the thing a quick death.
Good. I knew that we could count on you. It's simply a beast, but be cautious. The beasts of Skyrim are made of sterner stuff than most.
I knew we could count on you. Get it taken care of right away. Nobody wants an animal left roaming around in their place.
You're not afraid, are you? Of animals? Come back when you find your spine.
Unfortunate, but someone else will take care of it.
I see. Well, should you change your mind, the contract will be available for a while.
Orchendor? Kesh knew him. He is an overseer. Shepherd. Gathers the Afflicted, contains the festering wound.
Orchendor and his Afflicted are meant to stand ready, awaiting Peryite's command to cover the world with his Blessing.
Not far, not far. This one looks upon it on clear days. Look to the west, at the foot of the mountain. The dwarven ruins there. Bthardamz.
The pettiness of mortals. I had almost forgotten.
I can grant you a powerful token for this task. An escutcheon of Dwemer make, capable of shielding you from both the spell and sword.
Return when the elf lies dead and it will be yours.
An impertinent question. The elf must die. Either carry out my will or do not. You are not the only mortal agent I could choose.
So you have, mortal. Go now. Kill Orchendor.
I'm really getting tired of your excuses. When you borrowed the money, you said you'd pay it back on time and for double the usual fee.
Look, Shadr. Last warning. Pay up or else. All I care about is the gold. Everything else is your problem.
What? Are you telling me you robbed it? Why? Why are you doing this to me?
Next time, keep your plans quieter and nothing would have happened to it.
I know I did. But how was I to know the shipment would get robbed?
It is no matter, this fool will be the next act of the show.
And who is this?
I thought I was a goner just now. Thanks for the help, and if you need any supplies I have a few to sell.
Please, someone help me!
Help me! A vampire!
Movarth will taste your blood!
You will be my blood offering to Movarth!
Another victim fell for our ruse!
If you want something a bard can dig into look to the dragons.
A thousand years from now Skyrim will have changed rulers dozens of times but the return of the dragons, that story is once in an era.
I don't suppose it matters much anymore.
As long as Ulfric wishes Elisif to remain jarl, remain jarl she will. And the thanes will continue to follow her orders.
As long as those orders came from Ulfric.
Well... okay. My father owns the Winking Skeever. But it's the family business, so it'll be mine when he kicks off.
My father owned it until he... passed on.
I shouldn't be. Everyone wants to ask me about Mama and Uncle Roggvir.
Maybe if I don't talk, they won't either.
Thank you. I'm still at the beginning of my lessons. Dean Six Fingers used to say I was quite good with the flute.
You really don't know?
He opened the gate for Ulfric Stormcloak. Ulfric rides in, defeats the high king, and Roggvir was the one that let him ride right back out.
And now the Stormcloaks have claimed the entire city. I guess we have Roggvir to thank for that as well.
My husband. He was the blacksmith up at Castle Dour.
If you're looking for truth instead, see Noster. He's a good example of what life has to offer.
And if you're just looking to feel good, go get some Spiced Wine from Evette. But whatever you do, just leave me alone.
But you won't get any better than this from me.
Now that Sybille Stentor is out of the picture, I am indeed. And the court is, well... free of her... eccentricities.
Moving into her quarters was an interesting process.
Good man.
Stick around! Still plenty of songs to sing and mead to drink.
Yes. My older brother. He was a bit of a fop, really.
And truth be told, since Torygg's death, he had no real power in the court. But still... he didn't deserve to die.
What are you looking at?
Leave me alone.
I've got to get out of here.
It's been easier lately.
The Blackblood Marauders haven't been here since the Icerunner attack, so my shipments have been more regular.
I provide my wisdom and insight to the court.
It does indeed. You know, I hear you had something to do with Sybille Stentor's demise...
That must have been a hard bit of business. I hear Stentor had been alive for a long, long time.
No. Although it's true my husband Torygg was the high king and I am his widow, I am in no position to claim such a title.
Ulfric Stormcloak is victorious, and the jarls have fallen in line.
It seems I will never take my rightful place as high queen.
These days? Aye. Most of what I make is for folk like you.
But we must have the support of the other jarls for that claim to have any meaning.
Should they accept her rule, we will have peace.
Because killing her would yield no political advantage.
By leaving Elisif alive, Ulfric completes the separation of the Empire and Skyrim.
With Ulfric's troops here, Elisif will be forced to support his bid for high king. He secures the position and appears above reproach.
What's more, Ulfric's refusal to execute his slain enemy's wife will be portrayed as the ultimate act of compassion. Hmph. It's genius, really.
That's the real question, isn't it? No one can deny Elisif has a legitimate claim to become High Queen of Skyrim...
Well... By rights, she had a legitimate claim as High Queen of Skyrim.
But now Skyrim is at Ulfric's mercy.
The Moot will meet and choose Ulfric as high king. With his troops in most of the cities, they have little choice.
Perhaps, but necessary. As you know my cousin, Fura, died out there fighting for us. She wouldn't have had to, if it weren't for Roggvir.
Not much. But as a bard, I find the whole affair depressing.
There are no heroes in this war. Even with Ulfric here in Solitude, there are no winners to be had. No real conclusion.
You won't get anything out of me, you filthy sons o' horkers.
There's still Imperial camps to be cleaned up, as you know. And that takes arms and armor.
I swear, I left another bottle right here...
I got some fine armor, if you're lookin'.
Wanderer like you must have plenty of tales to tell. But I'm afraid I'm too busy to hear 'em.
Papa used to say I was greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him. I miss him...
So, what are your needs? Perhaps a pinch of this, and a bit of that? Hmm?
If you're looking for potions, I think I can be of service.
Beirand said you got here just before the execution. Must have been a lovely first sight.
Hmph. You know, you've got a certain... fire in your eyes. Just like my Beirand did. Please, feel free to look around. We've got a bit of everything.
Welcome. Look around. We've got everything you could want in life. At least... that's what my Beirand always told me...
I thought adventurers were supposed to look tough?
I bet my papa could have beat you up. He was stronger than anyone.
I get to help Mother with the shop. I used to help Papa too... till I tried to use the forge...
I can help with purchases, but if you've come to talk, see my sister. She's the one who gets on with people. It's her one virtue, really.
Have a drink, rest your feet and stay a little while.
I'm just running some errands for the old man... Err... For myself. I'm just running some errands for myself...
You don't want to talk to me. I'm just a stand in for a dead thane.
You get around, right? If you come across any Stormcloaks... gut one for me, huh? They did the same to my brother in Whiterun.
Think I'll forge me a new sword, just in case things go awry here in Solitude. I'm the blacksmith's apprentice, you see.
We've got strong swords and sturdy armor, if you're looking to buy.
Blacksmith, at your service.
